talkProposalPage.titlePart2: Share a talk with the community.
talkProposalPage.subtitle: If you have a talk you want to share with the Czech game dev community, send it our way.

insight.label: Insight
insight.body: We want concrete thinking, sharp takeaways, and ideas people can bring back into their own work.

energy.label: Presence
energy.body: A great talk does not need hype, but it does need conviction, rhythm, and a reason to be live.

clarity.label: Shape
clarity.body: If you can explain the arc clearly, we can help the talk find the right slot, room, and audience.

talkProposalPage.questions: Have questions about speaking? Email us at

editorial.title: We program talks that spark hallway conversations after the lights come back on.
editorial.body: That usually means practical case studies, unusual process breakdowns, failures worth unpacking, or a perspective the local scene does not hear often enough. You do not need to be polished. You do need a clear reason this talk should exist.
editorial.footer: Send the strongest version of the idea, even if the title is still rough.

whatWeLookFor.title: What We Look For
whatWeLookFor.subtitle: Signals that usually make a proposal click
whatWeLookFor.item1: Game development topics: programming, art, design, audio, production, business
whatWeLookFor.item4: Passion for sharing knowledge with the community

encouragement.title: Short talk, long talk, first talk, weird talk. Your voice matters.
encouragement.body1: You do not need years of speaker experience or a perfectly polished format.
encouragement.body2: A rough idea, a lesson from the work, or a topic you care about is enough to start. If you are excited to share it, we want to hear it.

process.title: Three short steps
process.step1: Send the core idea, the audience payoff, and the format that fits it best.
process.step2: We review for topic fit, clarity, and how the talk rounds out the event mix.
process.step3: If it feels right, we follow up about timing, framing, and where it belongs.

cards.signalBody: Tell us what is genuinely interesting, unusual, or useful about this talk.
cards.shapeBody: A rough format is enough. We mainly need to understand the size and pacing.
cards.fitBody: Location is flexible. We use it as a signal, not a hard constraint.

form.title: Pitch the talk
form.subtitle: A few short steps to tell us what you want to share.

speaker.title: Who is bringing this talk into the room?

talk.title: What is the sharpest version of the idea?

shape.title: How should the session be shaped?

form.talkTitlePlaceholder: What shipping our combat loop taught us about player trust
form.abstractPlaceholder: Tell us what happened in the project, what you learned, and what other game developers could take back to their own teams.
form.abstractHint: Minimum 50 characters. Strong abstracts usually cover the angle, the audience payoff, and what makes the story real.
form.duration15Description: Fast, focused, and great for one sharp idea.
form.duration30Description: Enough room for a clear story, examples, and takeaways.
form.duration45Description: Best for more technical topics or a fuller project breakdown.
form.locationEitherDescription: We are happy to place it where it fits best.
form.locationPragueDescription: Choose this if Prague feels like the right home for the talk.
form.locationZlinDescription: Choose this if Zlin feels like the right home for the talk.
form.experiencePlaceholder: For example: gameplay programmer on an indie release, tech artist on a live game, narrative designer on a student project...
form.experienceHint: Optional. First-time speakers are absolutely welcome.

speaker.title: About you
speaker.point1: Just the basics here. We only need to know who you are and how to reach you.
speaker.point2: If this is your first talk, that is completely fine.

talk.title: About the talk
talk.point1: The clearest abstracts usually explain what happened and what others can learn from it.
talk.point2: Working titles are fine. The core idea matters more.

shape.title: About the format
shape.point1: Short talks and deep dives are both welcome.
shape.point2: Pick the shape that fits the idea best. We can help refine it later.

followUp.title: If the idea is close
followUp.body: We may help tighten the framing, rebalance the length, or shift the emphasis before scheduling it.

form.successMessage: Thanks for sending it. We will review the proposal and follow up once we know how it fits the upcoming programming mix.
form.draftNote: Drafts are stored locally in your browser. If you don't submit, your data never reaches us.

errors.nameRequired: Please enter your name (at least 2 characters)
errors.emailInvalid: Please enter a valid email address
errors.titleRequired: Please enter a talk title (at least 5 characters)
errors.abstractRequired: Please provide an abstract (at least 50 characters)
errors.rateLimited: You've submitted too many proposals. Please try again later.
errors.botBlocked: We couldn't verify this request. Please try again.
errors.csrfInvalid: Please refresh the page and try again.
errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again or email us directly.
